news.summary: Occidental Petroleum Corp (NYSE: OXY) saw a rise in premarket trading after releasing its first-quarter results. The company reported an adjusted EPS of 87 cents per share, surpassing the analyst estimate of 77 cents. Quarterly revenue was $6.84 billion, slightly lower than the estimated $6.89 billion. The total company production was 1,391 thousand barrels of oil equivalent per day, with production averages for different regions: Permian - 754 Mboed, Rockies & Other Domestic - 292 Mboed, Gulf of America - 121 Mboed, and International - 224 Mboed. For further details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Occidental Petroleum beats Q1 profit forecast, cuts debt by $2.3B YTD. Revenue $6.84B, production 1,391 Mboed. Trading higher.

news.summary: In Boston, Bain Capital and Oliver Street Capital recently purchased an industrial portfolio in Northern New Jersey for a significant amount of $208 million. The acquisition was conducted privately through a collaboration between Bain Capital's Real Estate division and OSC, with a strategic focus on acquiring premium infill industrial properties in limited-supply markets. The portfolio, categorized as value-add, comprises 11 Class B warehouses situated in prime locations.
news.headline: Bain Capital and Oliver Street Capital jointly acquire 11 industrial properties in North Jersey market for $208M.

news.summary: In Chicago, Core Spaces is expanding its Build-to-Rent (BTR) brand, Oxenfree, which now includes nearly 3,000 homes in various fast-growing markets. The company has completed Oxenfree WeHo in Nashville's Wedgewood-Houston area. The brand's portfolio covers regions such as Dallas-Fort Worth, Denver, Nashville, Charlotte, Austin, Raleigh, and Florida's Gulf Coast. Core Spaces is a real estate developer, owner, and operator focused on driving growth in the housing market.
news.headline: Core Spaces has 3,000 new rental homes in operation, under development, or in the pipeline across various growing markets.

news.summary: A new global luxury real estate brand, Aperture Global Real Estate, was launched by LPT Holdings in Lake Mary, Florida. It is the first independent luxury brokerage to debut with a global launch at the start. Aperture enters the market with a groundbreaking international presence, starting in 15 U.S. states and four key international cities like Miami, London, New York, Toronto, and Lisbon. Additionally, the brand plans to expand to nearly 20 more global locations.
news.headline: Fast-growing US real estate firm, Aperture Global Real Estate, launches internationally in 15 states and 4 cities.

news.summary: KW MAPS Coaching, the coaching division of KW, recently revealed the launch of new training and educational courses overseen by Michael J. Maher in Austin, Texas. This update aims to provide valuable coaching resources to assist professionals in achieving their goals.
news.headline: Michael J. Maher will lead new training courses at KW MAPS Coaching, KW's coaching division.

news.summary: A civil engineering firm named Pape-Dawson has recently purchased Morris Engineering & Consulting, a Florida-based company. Established in 2009, Morris Engineering & Consulting specializes in land development services for the Sarasota area and Southwest Florida. The firm is known for its work on various projects such as residential developments, commercial sites, and large-scale master planned communities. Their team has built a solid reputation for providing dependable and efficient engineering solutions.
news.headline: Pape-Dawson purchases Morris Engineering & Consulting in Florida, known for land development services in Sarasota area; founded in 2009.

news.summary: In Pasadena, California on May 8, 2025, Landmark Theatres, known for its advancements in cinema, has partnered with HIA Technologies, a front-runner in AI-driven interactive video technology, to introduce an innovative digital platform called Landmark Loyalty. This collaboration aims to revolutionize the cinema experience through cutting-edge technology.
news.headline: Landmark Theatres and HIA Technologies launch 2-way interactive movie experience with Regan, Pasadena, May 8, 2025.

news.summary: In Indianapolis, IN on May 8, 2025, an event is being hosted by Remodel Health and Ambetter Health. This event will provide exclusive access to the Indy 500. Employers are increasingly adopting the individual coverage health reimbursement arrangement (ICHRA) as a modern alternative to traditional group health insurance plans. This shift is reflecting a significant trend in the healthcare industry, with a growing number of companies making this transition to benefit both themselves and their employees.
news.headline: AIM Conference on ICHRA market momentum and innovation is back on May 21-23 in Indianapolis, IN, with exclusive Indy 500 access.

news.summary: In New York, 2K and Hangar 13 announced the release of the Official Gameplay Trailer and ‘Breaking Omerta’ Developer Insights video for Mafia: The Old Country. The game is set to launch on August 8, 2025, for PlayStation®5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC via Steam and NVIDIA GeForce NOW. Fans can tune into the PAX East developer panel today at 2:00 p.m. EDT on YouTube and Twitch to gain insights from the developers themselves and pre-order the game. "Mafia: The Old Country" offers a focused experience for gamers.
news.headline: Mafia: The Old Country to launch on August 8, 2025 for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC. Gameplay trailer and dev insights released. PAX East panel at 2 p.m. EDT on YouTube and Twitch. Pre-orders open.

news.summary: A partnership has been formed between Gotransverse and The Whole Group in Austin, Texas, to provide automated billing and revenue management through the ServiceNow Subscription Order Management (SOM) application. This collaboration aims to streamline billing processes and enhance revenue tracking for businesses utilizing the platform. The partnership signifies a strategic move towards improved efficiency and financial management.
news.headline: Gotransverse and The Whole Group team up to offer billing and revenue automation on ServiceNow SOM app.

news.summary: In Las Vegas, Tanium, a top company in Autonomous Endpoint Management (AEM), is collaborating with ServiceNow to address key IT issues for clients. Through Tanium AEM for ServiceNow with ServiceNow® AI Agents, users can automatically address IT tickets, notably boosting employee satisfaction and operational effectiveness. A notable instance of their partnership is being unveiled at Knowledge 2025, ServiceNow's yearly event.
news.headline: Tanium AEM for ServiceNow improves IT operations for UKG, with AI Agents resolving tickets for better efficiency.

news.summary: IP Infusion has recently disclosed that Premium Choice Broadband, a fiber services provider in Maine, has enhanced its core network services by incorporating various IP Infusion software solutions and open networking hardware products. This transition signifies a change in Premium Choice's networking approach, having previously utilized Cisco core router products. Established in 2007, Premium Choice Broadband, part of the Varney family of companies, is a prominent player in the internet services industry.
news.headline: Premium Choice Broadband upgrades network with IP Infusion products from Cisco, shifting its networking strategy.Founded in 2007.

news.summary: In San Francisco, Amplitude (NASDAQ: AMPL), a top digital analytics platform, has revealed a strategic collaboration agreement with Amazon Web Services, Inc. (AWS) to boost their efforts in enhancing customer experiences. This agreement aims to create solutions that assist organizations in enhancing customer experiences, such as optimizing digital shopping journeys, improving user onboarding processes, and increasing viewer engagement. This collaboration comes at a time when organizations are inundated with data and still struggle to fully comprehend its implications.
news.headline: Amplitude forms collaboration with AWS to improve customer experiences through data solutions.

news.summary: In a significant development, the Nalu Real-World Data (RWD) paper has been published in the Chronic Pain and Management Journal, establishing Nalu peripheral nerve stimulation (PNS) as a crucial treatment for chronic pain. The analysis incorporates information from 2,273 patients who received the Nalu micro-IPG™ PNS System, marking it as the most extensive published real-world dataset for a permanent PNS device. With an impressive responder rate of 94%, this milestone emphasizes the effectiveness of Nalu PNS in addressing chronic pain.
news.headline: Nalu publishes largest real-world data report on permanent PNS device, including data from 2,273 patients with 94% responder rate.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, in London and New York, a consortium was established by major long-term institutional investors who hold significant sovereign bonds issued by Lebanon. This group, known as the Ad Hoc Group, was initiated in March 2020 due to the worsening financial and economic conditions in Lebanon.
news.headline: In May 2025, Ad Hoc Lebanon Bondholder Group was formed by institutional holders of sovereign bonds due to Lebanon's financial crisis.

news.summary: Jonathan Farnell, CEO of Freedx, discusses the challenges facing cryptocurrency adoption due to complicated interfaces and technical barriers, with only 560 million users worldwide currently. Streamlining user experience and focusing on simplicity are key to achieving mass adoption, potentially pushing the market cap to $4 trillion by 2025. He emphasizes the need for clear language, intuitive interfaces, and building trust through transparent feedback. Success in cryptocurrency adoption relies on usability and user-friendly design to attract everyday users and drive market growth. The article underscores the importance of focusing on benefits over features to make cryptocurrency a practical and widely-adopted financial tool.
news.headline: Cryptocurrency's success depends on user-friendly experiences. $2.91 trillion market cap could rise to $4 trillion in Q2 2025 with streamlined UX.

news.summary: EPAM Systems, Inc. revealed its first-quarter 2025 financial outcomes, surpassing expectations. The company's sales climbed by 11.7% year-over-year to $1.302 billion, exceeding the projected $1.28 billion. On an organic constant currency basis, revenue saw a 1.4% YoY increase. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) were reported at $2.41, lower than the previous year's $2.56 but higher than the estimated $2.27. Adjusted operating income for the quarter saw a rise to $175.8 million, up by 1.2% YoY. However, the margin experienced a decline from 14.9% YoY to 13.5%. The operating cash flow for the period in review also saw a decrease. Bank of America suggests forgetting rate cuts after Powell's hawkish tone. Curious to know more? The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: EPAM Systems Inc. (NYSE: EPAM) beats Q1 analyst sales estimate, with $1.302B revenue. Adjusted EPS of $2.41 beats estimates.

news.summary: In Hamamatsu, Japan, Yamaha Corporation (TOKYO: 7951) has disclosed the launch of investments from Yamaha Music Innovations Fund I, LP (YMIF), a corporate venture capital (CVC) fund managed by U.S. subsidiary Yamaha Music Innovations, LLC. President and CEO Yusuke “Scott” Sugino will lead this initiative, which involves tapping into a dedicated fund worth US$50 million starting from May 2025. With a focus on solidifying its pioneering role in sound and music innovation, Yamaha has established a representative office to drive business development.
news.headline: Yamaha Corp. to invest $50 million in startup companies through its venture capital fund in Silicon Valley.
